sprint: Cinematic Polish Sprint -- Traveller Gateway V1
task: A deliberate no-new-functionality pass making the Sky-to-Hall transition (Scenes 4-6) feel like entering another world instead of a UI transition, per a complete creative brief scoped around seven named cinematic principles. Scenes 1-3 (Sky/Lumo's Arrival/Identity) were confirmed already working and left untouched. Principle 1 (the Gate is REVEALED, never appears): four real sequenced beats -- wind rises, a golden halo glows behind the still-hidden gate, curtains part onto a dark silhouette, the silhouette brightens into real stonework with gathering light particles. Principle 2 (the Traveller moves, not the Gate): Journey gained genuine parallax depth layers (near/far streaming clouds, islands, birds) so the world visibly moves around a Gate that only grows modestly here. Principle 3 (Lumo leads): a real multi-leg staged flight -- fly a leg, glance back, hold, continue, repeat. Principle 4 (the Gate feels ancient/massive): a new Arrival beat grows the gate to dominate the screen while Lumo settles clear of it. Principle 5 (build anticipation, never rush): a real, verified Pause beat with nothing new happening, before Lumo raises one wing and the gate runes ignite with real magic sparks. Principle 6 (cross the threshold): the doors' slow open now leads into a real forward camera dolly before the final golden flash, not straight to it. Principle 7 (protect the mystery): the Hall is never visible before completion, unchanged. Environmental polish (haze wash, swaying light rays, extra wind streaks/islands/birds) layered behind the existing sky decorations. Audio's ambient-layer request was investigated and deliberately left as a disclosed gap, since it conflicts with the established no-autoplay audio policy. A real, screenshot-caught composition bug was found and fixed: Lumo's centered rest position collided with the Gate's own centered reveal anchor during the Reveal beat -- fixed with a new yield step that has Lumo step aside first, with an initial over-correction (which hid Lumo behind the massive Gate at Arrival) caught and corrected in the same pass so only the flight animation's early stops depart from the yield position while the Arrival composition is restored to its original, validated values. Verified via a 24-assertion Playwright suite covering both the Traveller and Returning Creator paths end-to-end plus 8 real screenshots at every key beat and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to css/style.css, js/gatewaySequence.js, and index.html's ambient decoration markup.
